title: Colab reproduction suite — pre-registered expected results (run elsewhere, verify here)
protocol: colab_suite.py is self-contained (paste into Google Colab). It prints RESULTS_SHA256 over environment-STABLE quantities. A faithful run anywhere reproduces the expected_results_sha256 below. Raw LMD distances carry cross-BLAS float noise and are DISPLAYED but excluded from the hash.

tests.T1_lmd_ring: LMD ring-lattice coupler sweep -> log-log slope EXACTLY -0.5, R^2=1.0 (algebraic; matches the user's Colab: slope -0.500000, R^2 1.000000).
tests.T2_lmd_metric: effective-resistance distance is a metric -> 0 triangle violations / 100 random graphs.
tests.T3_lism: E=U*D linear beats quadratic on a seeded cohort (R2 lin > quad).
tests.T5_echo: Echo hash-chain Merkle root is fixed; a one-record tamper changes the root.
tests.T6_tau_v: failed repos have higher enforcement latency; Mann-Whitney separates them.

acceptance: A returned Colab RESULTS_SHA256 that EQUALS expected_results_sha256 = independent reproduction confirmed. A mismatch is investigated field-by-field (verify_colab.py) -- an honest divergence (e.g., a numpy/BLAS issue on a stable field, or a real code change) is reported, not hidden.
honest_scope: This suite covers the DETERMINISTIC, portable core of the stack (LMD, LISM, swarm, Echo Merkle, tau_v, Hoffman). HELM/PAGES/EI-LLM use JS-specific RNG/regex and are verified in-repo via node (reproduce_all.sh); their Python-portable governance logic is a future addition to this suite.
